name: Samir Katte
education: Master's Degree, Electrical Engineering from UCLA; Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.), Electronics and TeleCommunications Engineering from University of Mumbai
headline: Senior Software Engineer | Ex-Qualcomm | UCLA Alumni
about:
I got into engineering because I am fascinated by the innovative nature of this field. Every few years there is a new technology which gives me an opportunity to learn and become a better engineer by enhancing my skillset.

I love being part of path breaking technologies like 5G and ML/AI. Currently I work in the MAIA100 AI chip team at Microsoft. Previously I worked at Qualcomm as a RFSW Engineer, and I have written Modem Software for 3G/4G/5G technologies on multiple generations of smartphones over 7+ years. Before that I graduated from UCLA with a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering.

In the past I have successfully completed projects by working in a team or as an individual contributor. I am always ready to learn new technical stuff and that makes me flexible to work in changing environments. I have experience taking up technical leadership roles too.

I look forward to a bright career in the field of ML/AI in the upcoming years.

currentPositions.description:
Developing code to implement software features for programming MAIA100 chip in the Azure cloud.
MAIA100 is Microsoft's first in-house AI accelerator for training and inferencing various large models for Deep Learning applications.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Developing code to implement software features for programming MAIA100 chip in the Azure cloud.
MAIA100 is Microsoft's first in-house AI accelerator for training and inferencing various large models for Deep Learning applications.

experiences.title: Staff RF Embedded Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Led the MMW 5G Device Driver Team to deliver software solutions on the Snapdragon X60 modem platform.
•	Designed new software features in MMW driver and debugged various advanced RF issues for different OEMs.
experiences.jobLocation: San Diego County, California, United States

experiences.title: Senior RF Embedded Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Worked on software design of the device driver to achieve 5G millimeter wave radio communication in GHz range.
•	Implemented various MMW features in software for supporting Snapdragon X55 modem platform.
•	Previously developed software driver for LTE 4G and legacy technologies to program the analog chip modules for call processing.
•	Resolved a variety of customer issues to help deliver quality software releases for flagship phone launches.

experiences.title: RF Embedded Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Developed LTE software driver for various advanced throughput features on uplink and downlink radio channels (Physical layer).
•	Enhanced CDMA signal performance on multiple generations of modems for more than 2 years in the technology team.

experiences.companyName: UCLA Smart Grid Energy Research Center (SMERC)
experiences.title: Graduate Student Researcher
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Worked on embedded platforms such as Arduino Uno and Raspberry Pi for implementing Machine to Machine interface on appliances like an electric dryer
•	Created circuits for energy metering applications and for extracting sensor data from appliances
•	Implemented communication protocols like SPI, I2C and ZigBee for integrating appliances with the main power grid 
•	Worked also on embedded circuit design for controlling electric dryers remotely

educations.title: University of Mumbai
educations.subtitle: Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.), Electronics and TeleCommunications Engineering

licenseAndCertificates.name: Deep Learning A-Z 2025: Neural Networks, AI & ChatGPT Prize

publications.title: A Survey Report on Hardware Trojan Detection by Multiple-Parameter Side-Channel Analysis
Bus Tracking and Ticketing System using GPS and GSM Technology
publications.summary: Implemented an ATMEGA640 microcontroller based system which tracks the location of the bus arriving at the bus stop using a GPS module and calculates its expected time of arrival. Created an Android application through which the commuters can see the current status of the buses arriving at a bus stop and plan their route accordingly. They can also book their tickets in advance through their cell phones.

publications.title: Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) using Zigbee Technology and its Advantages
publications.summary:
•	Integration of the ZigBee protocol for bidirectional communication between individual meters and central processors allowing automated meter readings with timestamps
•	Detection of electricity thefts using an innovative method in the power grid

publications.title: Wireless SCADA technology using GPRS
publications.summary:
•	Implementation of a  wireless SCADA system employing a GSM module at the location of the system capturing vital data
•	A normal cell phone is used in the infrastructure to monitor the status of the particular system under observation by wireless communication

recommendationsReceived.name: Krishna Kamath
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Applied Scienst at Amazon
recommendationsReceived.context: Krishna worked with Samir on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I have known Samir both personally and professionally during my graduate study at UCLA. As we worked in same research lab, I have seen Samir in multiple group meeting and was excellent in communicating his ideas and plan of execution to the rest of the team. I have also seen him handling lab research projects along with the graduate course work and projects. He is a hard working, very responsible person to have in the team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Dr. Vishakha Kelkar
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Associate Professor at D J Sanghvi College Of Engineering
recommendationsReceived.description: I have had a chance to closely interact with Samir as a professor of subjects like ‘Principles of Communication Engineering’, ‘Random Signal Analysis’, ‘Neural Networks and Fuzzy Logic’ and ‘Data Compression and Encryption’ during his Engineering. Samir has been very attentive during the lectures and always made an attempt to grasp as much as possible. He showed great programming skills during the practical work that he performed as a part of the curriculum.  He has always been polite as well as soft spoken and has continually tried to learn as much as possible, thus striving for excellence in all his subjects. I would highly recommend him as a deserving candidate in any area he decided to pursue.

recommendationsReceived.name: Hyunsoo Choe
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: SDE at Amazon
recommendationsReceived.description: Samir is diligent and responsible. When we worked on a project together, he kept making brilliant idea and he showed professional output. He had deep understanding of network such as wireless and signals. Also, he was able to utilize programming languages skillfully.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jay Khandhar
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Software Engineering Manager at Apple
recommendationsReceived.description:
I worked with Samir on many projects during my undergrad at University of Mumbai, D.J. Sanghvi College of Engineering. 

He has good technical skills and pays attention to detail. We worked together on projects involving Micro-controller Development boards and sensors. It was a pleasure to work with him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Shaun Hogan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Electrical Engineer
recommendationsReceived.description: Samir was a pleasure to work with at all times.  His reliability and professionalism contributed to a positive work environment and his technical knowledge ensured that our project was done correctly and on time.

recommendations.name: Hyunsoo Choe
recommendations.subtitle: SDE at Amazon
recommendations.description: I worked with Hyunsoo during a course project at UCLA. He has impressive programming skills and his work experience adds professionalism to the entire team when he works on a project.

recommendations.name: Jay Khandhar
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineering Manager at Apple
recommendations.description: I worked with Jay during several undergrad course projects. Jay is a very hardworking person.and his programming skills while dealing with embedded systems are really impressive. His capability of performing well under pressure and tackling difficult problems, makes him a great person to work with.

recommendations.name: Shaun Hogan
recommendations.subtitle: Electrical Engineer
recommendations.description: Shaun is a very hardworking person. I worked with him during a course project and his in-depth knowledge of the subject matter is really impressive. His problem-solving skills made sure that out team fulfilled the needs of the project before the deadlines.

summary: Samir Katte is currently a Microsoft professional who frames his LinkedIn presence around enterprise AI, cloud infrastructure, and broader industry developments. Working at a large, public-stage company, he highlights major product and platform milestones (for example, Microsoft Azure and the MAIA100 AI Accelerator) while also amplifying open source and community news like the PyTorch Foundation. His posts are concise, upbeat, and often celebratory — mixing short commentary, curated article shares, and hashtag-driven signals rather than deep technical treatises. He demonstrates a clear interest in AI/ML infrastructure, deep learning hardware (TPUs/accelerators), developer ecosystems, and networking trends such as 5G, showing a breadth of industry awareness. Samir frequently acknowledges teams and organizational achievements, signaling community orientation and internal/external engagement. Overall, his content provides timely industry commentary, enterprise product enthusiasm, and active support for open-source and developer communities, making him a credible amplifier of AI and cloud infrastructure news.
